D3. 默认全 deepseek 家族

drafts.title: Legacy search used token TF-IDF plus boosts
drafts.compiledTruth: The pre-LLM memory_search pipeline enumerated files, parsed entries, tokenized title/slug/compiled_truth, applied TF-IDF plus substring boosts, multiplied by confidence and project boost, then returned top-N non-archived entries.

drafts.title: Literal token search fails mixed-language memory
drafts.compiledTruth: A memory corpus with CJK text, English variants, trigger phrases, and timelines cannot rely on literal token matching: continuous Chinese may become one unusable token, morphology variants miss, trigger/timeline fields disappear, and substring boosts become brittle.

drafts.title: Search quality controls sediment dedupe quality
drafts.compiledTruth: When sediment dedupe uses memory_search as its lookup kernel, weak semantic recall directly produces duplicate or near-duplicate entries because old equivalent knowledge is not surfaced before create/update decisions.

drafts.title: Patching grep cannot solve semantic equivalence
drafts.compiledTruth: Adding trigger phrases, CJK bigrams, stemming, or timeline fields to grep/TF-IDF improves literal recall but still cannot reliably find semantic equivalents such as cross-language paraphrases or differently worded durable truths.
drafts.sourceHeading: 已被审查否决的替代方案

drafts.title: Whole-vault LLM recall does not scale
drafts.compiledTruth: Putting every entry summary into one LLM call can be accurate at small corpus size, but it creates uncontrolled token growth and breaks down as the memory vault grows toward 1000+ entries.
drafts.sourceHeading: 已被审查否决的替代方案

drafts.title: memory_search accepts natural language prompts
drafts.compiledTruth: memory_search keeps its external SearchResult schema stable while broadening query semantics from keywords to natural-language retrieval prompts; callers do not need to know that LLM retrieval is used internally.
drafts.sourceHeading: D1. 接口不变内部走 LLM

drafts.title: Two-stage search separates recall from precision
drafts.compiledTruth: LLM memory search should use Stage 1 for broad high-recall classification over the whole candidate surface, then Stage 2 for high-precision reranking over full entries fetched by slug.

drafts.title: Stage 1 uses full-body candidate surface
drafts.compiledTruth: Runtime Stage 1 no longer depends on frontmatter-only _index.md; it generates a full_body_v3 surface from parsed entries including summary, trigger phrases, related slugs, compiled truth, and timeline with per-entry truncation protection.

drafts.title: Stage 2 always reranks candidates
drafts.compiledTruth: If Stage 1 returns any candidates, Stage 2 must run even when the candidate count is small; skipping precision rerank weakens relevance quality and defeats the two-stage retrieval contract.

drafts.title: Rerank is relevance judgment, not deep reasoning
drafts.compiledTruth: Memory reranking is mostly reading comprehension and relevance judgment; timeline evidence such as supersession is a stated fact to read, not a reasoning chain that benefits from expensive thinking mode.
drafts.sourceHeading: D3. 默认全 deepseek 家族

drafts.title: Default search models are DeepSeek flash off
drafts.compiledTruth: Both Stage 1 and Stage 2 default to deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash with thinking=off because recall/rerank are high-volume semantic classification tasks where flash has the best latency/quality fit and thinking adds no ranking benefit.
drafts.sourceHeading: D3. 默认全 deepseek 家族

drafts.title: Search models are settings-configurable
drafts.compiledTruth: Stage 1 model, Stage 1 limit, Stage 1 thinking, Stage 2 model, Stage 2 limit, and Stage 2 thinking belong in settings so environments can trade latency, provider availability, language quality, and model diversity without changing callers.

drafts.title: Result cache breaks memory freshness
drafts.compiledTruth: Do not cache final memory_search result lists by query: sediment writes can happen between repeated searches, and a cached slug list would hide freshly written entries that should immediately be recallable.
drafts.sourceHeading: D6. 不做 result-level 缓存

drafts.title: Fresh search surface preserves new entry recall
drafts.compiledTruth: Each memory_search run should generate its candidate surface from the currently parsed entries, so newly created or updated memory is eligible for Stage 1 immediately without waiting for cache expiry.
drafts.sourceHeading: D6. 不做 result-level 缓存

drafts.title: Search results show freshness, not full timeline
drafts.compiledTruth: Search result cards should expose created, updated, rank_reason, and only a short timeline tail; full timelines remain available through memory_get to avoid polluting the main search context.
drafts.sourceHeading: D8. 新鲜度与时间精度

drafts.title: Memory timestamps need local datetime precision
drafts.compiledTruth: New sediment entries should use local ISO datetime timestamps rather than date-only values because multiple same-day entries may supersede or rapidly correct one another and require precise ordering.
drafts.sourceHeading: D8. 新鲜度与时间精度

drafts.title: memory_search is sediment lookup kernel
drafts.compiledTruth: Before creating sediment, the curator should query memory_search for semantically related old entries and use returned relevance, timeline, and freshness evidence to decide update, merge, skip, or create.
drafts.sourceHeading: D7. 为 sediment curator 提供 lookup 内核

drafts.title: Sediment dedupe evolves knowledge
drafts.compiledTruth: Semantic-neighbor lookup should not collapse to duplicate-equals-reject; a curator needs operation choices such as update, merge, skip, and create so memory evolves instead of only appending or rejecting.
drafts.sourceHeading: D7. 为 sediment curator 提供 lookup 内核

drafts.title: Instructions-first prompts enable prefix KV cache
drafts.compiledTruth: Stage 1 and Stage 2 prompts should put stable instructions and index/candidate material before the query so providers with prefix-level KV caching can reuse repeated prefix tokens across calls.

drafts.title: KV cache is not result cache
drafts.compiledTruth: Provider prefix KV cache reuses computation for token prefixes, not the LLM output slug list; it can reduce latency/cost without hiding newly written entries because changed candidate surfaces naturally change the prompt prefix.

drafts.title: Full-body Stage 1 prioritizes recall
drafts.compiledTruth: The move from frontmatter-only Stage 1 input to full_body_v3 intentionally sacrifices prompt compactness and some cache efficiency because recall accuracy is more important than old prompt-cache optimization.

drafts.title: search-metrics.jsonl records call observability
drafts.compiledTruth: Each memory_search call appends a best-effort JSONL metrics row under the project .pi-astack memory directory with timestamp, query prefix, Stage 1/2 token/cache stats when available, result count, verdict, surface version, and path marker.

drafts.title: Search metrics schema is experimental
drafts.compiledTruth: search-metrics.jsonl is experimental: its schema is not frozen, provider cache fields are present only when reported, writes are best-effort and swallowed on failure, and aggregation tooling remains an open question.
